key_insight: Viscoelastic droplets relax and fuse through distinct capillary-driven mechanisms, with stress relaxation introducing a second timescale that can strongly alter intermediate fusion dynamics compared with Newtonian droplets.
engineering_relevance: A unified viscoelastic model is essential for predicting condensate and soft-matter shape evolution, especially when fusion rates and recovery times control material function or processing.
method: Analytical small-deformation theory for droplet shape recovery
Axisymmetric finite-element simulations using the Oldroyd-B constitutive model
Comparison of shape recovery, fusion stages, and stretched-exponential fits across Newtonian and viscoelastic regimes
evidence_signal: The paper compares theory and simulations across Newtonian and viscoelastic cases, showing multistage fusion behavior, Deborah-number dependence, and deviations from stretched-exponential empirical predictions in viscoelastic regimes.
watch_item: Oldroyd-B modeling may not capture all real condensate rheology, and added exterior-fluid dissipation can significantly slow fusion, so boundary conditions and surrounding-medium effects must be included carefully.
paper_title: Droplet Fusion as a Relaxation Process: Comparison with Shape Recovery of Newtonian and Viscoelastic Droplets
